main_sections.title: Madhu's Wish
main_sections.prose_summary: Madhu, a boy who loves eating all kinds of food, sits under a banyan tree every day at noon for lunch. One day an old man arrives; Madhu shares his food. The grateful old man grants Madhu a wish from the magical tree. Madhu wishes for different types of food forever, ignoring the tree's gentle warning to think carefully. The tree grants laddoos, then fruits (grapes, mangoes, apples, bananas, jackfruit, melons, guavas, oranges), then savouries (matri, samosas, kachori, potato chips, vada). Madhu gets hiccups and desperately needs water, but the tree reminds him he wished for food only — not water. Madhu tries sharbat, kheer, and badam milk but the hiccups persist and worsen. He pleads for water, renouncing all the food. The tree gives him a jug of water, his hiccups stop, and he learns the importance of water above all other food. Story attributed to Sudha Murty. Theme: the value of water, the consequence of hasty wishes, kindness and its rewards. Tone: gently humorous, didactic.

reading.section_name: Let us read + Let us think

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us read: [Read the story 'Madhu's Wish' — prose narrative, pp. 88–92.]

What do you think Madhu will do now? [Prediction prompt, p.89]
What do you think will happen now? [Prediction prompt, p.90]

question_bank.stem: What do you think Madhu will do now?
What do you think will happen now?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us think.
A. Answer the following.
1. What did Madhu enjoy?
2. Do you think the old man had magical powers? How do you know?
3. Why did the tree ask Madhu to 'think over' his wish?
4. Name some of the things that Madhu ate.

question_bank.stem: What did Madhu enjoy?
Do you think the old man had magical powers? How do you know?
Why did the tree ask Madhu to 'think over' his wish?
Name some of the things that Madhu ate.

oracy.section_name: Let us think + Let us listen

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us think.
B. Think and say.
1. Have you experienced hiccups?
2. What do you do when you get hiccups?
3. Do you think water is important? Why do you think so?
4. If you were in Madhu's place, what would you wish for and why?

question_bank.stem: Have you experienced hiccups?
What do you do when you get hiccups?
Do you think water is important? Why do you think so?
If you were in Madhu's place, what would you wish for and why?

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us listen.
A. Your teacher will read parts of the story. Listen carefully and tell the class what happened after that.
1. Madhu said, 'Oh tree, let me have different types of food. Nothing else.'
2. As he was eating, he suddenly got hiccups. He wanted water.

question_bank.stem: Your teacher reads: 'Madhu said, Oh tree, let me have different types of food. Nothing else.' What happened after that?
Your teacher reads: 'As he was eating, he suddenly got hiccups. He wanted water.' What happened after that?

tasks_verbatim.transcript_text: Part 1: Madhu said, 'Oh tree, let me have different types of food. Nothing else.' The tree granted the wish. First, a basket of laddoos appeared. Then colourful fruits in baskets appeared. He started eating. / Part 2: As he was eating, he suddenly got hiccups. He wanted water. He looked around. There was food everywhere but no water. He asked for water but the tree said, 'You wanted only food not water.'

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us write.
A. Complete the following story.
Vijaya is a young girl. She lives with her father, mother, grandmother and grandfather in a small village.
Each day _________________________________________________
[Students continue the story in the space provided.]

question_bank.stem: Complete the story: 'Vijaya is a young girl. She lives with her father, mother, grandmother and grandfather in a small village. Each day …'

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us write.
A. Read the incomplete sentences. Discuss with your friends and complete them.
1. Madhu shared his food with the old man because ___________
2. Madhu was happy because ___________
3. After eating fruits, Madhu asked for savouries because ___________
4. Madhu asked for water because ___________

question_bank.stem: Complete: Madhu shared his food with the old man because ___.
Complete: Madhu was happy because ___.
Complete: After eating fruits, Madhu asked for savouries because ___.
Complete: Madhu asked for water because ___.

word_work.section_name: Let us learn + Fun with Words

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us learn.
A. Madhu enjoyed the following items. Write them in the correct basket.
Help Box: banana, kheer, melon, guava, kachori, potato chips, laddoo, vada, jackfruit, samosa
Baskets: Fruits | Sweets | Salty snacks

question_bank.stem: Classify the following items into the correct basket — Fruits, Sweets, or Salty snacks: banana, kheer, melon, guava, kachori, potato chips, laddoo, vada, jackfruit, samosa.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us learn.
B. Read the following sentences.
1. Madhu shared his food with the old man. He is kind.
2. Madhu wanted to eat everything. He is greedy.
3. The old man gave a special gift to Madhu. He is generous.
[Introduction of adjectives describing character.]

C. Choose a word from the box to describe the people below.
brave   polite   selfish   lazy
1. Pramod doesn't share his toys with anyone. He is ____________________.
2. The woman saved the boy from the robber. She is ____________________.
3. Advait is always lying in bed and doing nothing. She is ____________________.
4. Prerna always wishes everyone in the morning. She is ____________________.

question_bank.stem: Choose the correct word (brave, polite, selfish, lazy): Pramod doesn't share his toys with anyone. He is ___.
Choose the correct word (brave, polite, selfish, lazy): The woman saved the boy from the robber. She is ___.
Choose the correct word (brave, polite, selfish, lazy): Advait is always lying in bed and doing nothing. She is ___.
Choose the correct word (brave, polite, selfish, lazy): Prerna always wishes everyone in the morning. She is ___.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us learn.
D. Read the following sentences on combining sentences using 'and' or 'but'.
[Examples: 'Sam is watching television. Pratham is watching television.' → 'Sam and Pratham are watching television.' / 'Sam works in the afternoon. Pratham works at night.' → 'Sam works in the afternoon but Pratham works at night.']

Rewrite the following sentences using 'and' or 'but'.
1. I eat an apple. I eat an orange. I eat a peach. (and)
2. I like to watch cartoons. My sister likes to watch movies. (but)
3. Anita plays volleyball. Anju plays volleyball. (and)
4. Dolma wants a new comic book. She has spent all her pocket money on sweets. (but)
5. Rajesh wants to go to the park. He has not finished his homework. (but)

question_bank.stem: Rewrite using 'and': I eat an apple. I eat an orange. I eat a peach.
Rewrite using 'but': I like to watch cartoons. My sister likes to watch movies.
Rewrite using 'and': Anita plays volleyball. Anju plays volleyball.
Rewrite using 'but': Dolma wants a new comic book. She has spent all her pocket money on sweets.
Rewrite using 'but': Rajesh wants to go to the park. He has not finished his homework.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Fun with Words.
B. Notice that the first letter of each word has the same letter.
[Examples given — alliterative pyramid: 'Carrots / Cut Carrots / Chandu Cut Carrots' (C) and 'Apples / Ate Apples / Anu Ate Apples' (A).]
The first letter of each word is ____.
The first letter of each word is ____.

C. Write down similar words as shown above using your name or any name of your choice.
The first letter of each word is ____.

question_bank.stem: Identify the first letter shared by each word in the alliterative pyramid: 'Carrots / Cut Carrots / Chandu Cut Carrots'. The first letter of each word is ___.
Identify the first letter shared by each word in the alliterative pyramid: 'Apples / Ate Apples / Anu Ate Apples'. The first letter of each word is ___.
Write your own alliterative pyramid using your name or a name of your choice. The first letter of each word is ___.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Let us do.
Let us make a table mat.
Materials needed: An A4 sheet paper, pictures of various fruits and vegetables, bits and pieces of coloured papers, glue and a pencil or a sketch pen.
Steps:
1. On the A4 sheet, draw fruits or vegetables of your choice like an apple, a mango or a pear.
2. Take the sheet of red coloured paper. Make small bits by tearing it.
3. Stick the bits of papers on the apple picture you made.
4. Likewise, stick the yellow paper or the colour you like in the mango picture.
5. You can cover it with any plastic sheet available in your house.
6. Your table mat is ready!

question_bank.stem: Make a table mat using an A4 sheet: draw fruits/vegetables, tear coloured paper into small pieces, and paste them to fill in the fruit outlines. Cover with a plastic sheet to finish.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Six step-by-step illustrations showing: (1) A4 sheet with drawn apple and mango outlines, (2) tearing red paper into small bits, (3) sticking red bits onto apple, (4) sticking yellow bits onto mango, (5) covering with plastic sheet, (6) finished table mat with colourful fruit mosaics
